items.q: What does a 'large scale' map mean?
items.correct: A large-scale map covers a small area in high detail (e.g. 1:25,000 OS map of one town); a small-scale map covers a large area in less detail (e.g. 1:1,000,000 world atlas).
items.misconception: A large-scale map shows a large area.
items.random_wrong: Large scale means the map is physically big.
items.why: This is counter-intuitive but mathematically logical: 1/25,000 is a larger fraction than 1/1,000,000. Students reverse the terminology and consistently mark themselves down on map skill questions.

items.q: What is hydraulic action in a river?
items.correct: The force of moving water itself compressing air into cracks in the river bank or bed, weakening rock until pieces break off — no sediment needed.
items.misconception: Hydraulic action is when rocks in the river smash into each other.
items.random_wrong: Hydraulic action is when the river dissolves rocks chemically.
items.why: Students mix up the four river erosion processes. Hydraulic action = water force; abrasion = water using rocks to scrape; attrition = rocks hitting rocks; solution (corrosion) = chemical dissolving.

items.q: Why do informal settlements develop in rapidly growing LIC cities?
items.correct: Rural-urban migration outpaces formal housing supply; migrants build their own homes on marginal land without secure land tenure — driven by housing shortage, not by personal choice.
items.misconception: People live in slums because they prefer them or are too lazy to find better housing.
items.random_wrong: Informal settlements are built by the government for new migrants.
items.why: Self-construction is a response to formal-sector failure. Many residents are employed and pay informal rent. Students moralise; the geographical analysis is about supply, tenure and land economics.
The answer examiners want is: Emergent. “Canopy” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly what % of species live in rainforests?
items.why: Correct answer: Over 50%. “10%” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
